verificationLabel: Round 8 team lists checked Wednesday 22 Apr 2026
summary: Round 8 resets the board after the Tigers charity miss, with Bulldogs the cleanest straight tip on team news while several other games still need price discipline.

scoreboard.note: The Tigers get Starford To'a into the backline while the Raiders lose Hudson Young and Josh Papalii, with Simi Sasagi also ruled out by a hamstring strain.
North Queensland welcome back Reed Mahoney, Murray Taulagi and Jeremiah Nanai, but Cronulla get Cam McInnes back and still look a little cleaner on balance.
Stephen Crichton returns for Canterbury while Brisbane still face life without Payne Haas and Corey Jensen up front.
The Roosters still look steadier than a Dragons side carrying suspension and injury noise through the spine and outside backs.
Charnze Nicoll-Klokstad is out, which dulls the Warriors edge, but home ground still keeps them narrowly in front on the board.
Melbourne have stuck with the same 19-man squad despite five straight losses, while Souths arrive with more trust in tight games.
Penrith lose Mitch Kenny but still have Nathan Cleary steering the side, while Newcastle remain short of their best outside backs.
Turbo is out with a hamstring injury, but Manly are otherwise settled and still look more convincing than Parramatta.

liveAlerts.text: The Round 7 Tigers charity bet lost 21-20 to Brisbane, so the Mark Hughes Foundation bank slips back to $6.50.
Bulldogs over Broncos looks like the cleanest straight tip, with Crichton back and Brisbane still missing Payne Haas.
Cowboys versus Sharks is one of the hardest games to price because both sides get major reinforcements back.
Turbo's hamstring injury changes the Manly-Eels matchup more than any other Round 8 team-list move.
Storm have dropped five straight and still cannot be trusted blindly, even at home.
No Round 8 charity bet is locked yet, because the cleanest side may still be priced too short to be worth it.
